recentIncidents.name: IBM Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud - Planning Analytics Workspace Service Outage in EU region - RESOLVED
recentIncidents.body: Resolved 06/17/2026 10:11 AM UTC The issue impacting customer in Europe region has been resolved. Services are operating normally. Our team has completed monitoring and confirmed service stability. We apologize for any inconvenience and thank you for your patience. Updated 06/17/2026 8:58 AM UTC Dear Customer, IBM has observed a brief interruption affecting one of the planning analytics workspace services. Our teams are currently investigating and working to restore normal functionality. Customers in EU region can may encounter problems accessing their environments. We are still working on resolving the issue. Thank you for your patience. Kind regards, Planning Analytics on Workspace Team Updated 06/17/2026 8:25 AM UTC Dear Customer, IBM has observed a brief interruption affecting one of the planning analytics workspace services. Our teams are currently investigating and working to restore normal functionality. We are still working on resolving the issue. Thank you for your patience. Kind regards, Planning Analytics on Workspace Team Published 06/17/2026 7:18 AM UTC Dear Customer, IBM has observed a brief interruption affecting one of the planning analytics workspace services. Our teams are currently investigating and working to restore normal functionality. Thank you for your patience. Kind regards, Planning Analytics on Workspace Team

recentIncidents.name: Login Issues - Singapore Region
recentIncidents.body: Resolved 06/08/2026 4:44 AM UTC The issue impacting customer logins in the Singapore region has been resolved. Services are operating normally. Our team has completed monitoring and confirmed service stability. We apologize for any inconvenience and thank you for your patience. Updated 06/08/2026 3:16 AM UTC The issue impacting environment logins in the Singapore region has been resolved. Our team is continuing to monitor the services to ensure stability. Published 06/08/2026 2:34 AM UTC We are currently investigating an issue impacting customer logins in the Singapore region. The issue may impact Planning Analytics environments and Controller environments that are integrated with Planning Analytics. Our team is actively working to identify the cause and restore normal operations. We will provide further updates as soon as more information becomes available.

recentIncidents.name: File Transfer Services and Drive Explorer Functionality Outage – EU Region - RESOLVED
recentIncidents.body: Resolved 06/05/2026 12:55 PM UTC We have successfully re‑established the File Transfer service in our Europe region. The service is now operational. Thank you for your continued patience as we complete the remaining recovery activities. Published 06/05/2026 12:22 PM UTC This email is to advise you that we are currently investigating an unplanned interruption to EU File Transfer Service. Our apologies for this inconvenience. We are investigating and will provide further update every 1 hour.

incident_updates.body: Resolved 06/05/2026 12:55 PM UTC We have successfully re‑established the File Transfer service in our Europe region. The service is now operational. Thank you for your continued patience as we complete the remaining recovery activities. Published 06/05/2026 12:22 PM UTC This email is to advise you that we are currently investigating an unplanned interruption to EU File Transfer Service. Our apologies for this inconvenience. We are investigating and will provide further update every 1 hour.

recentIncidents.name: IBM Controller on Cloud - Controller Web UI login issue
recentIncidents.body: Updated 05/19/2026 5:43 PM UTC Dear Customer, The issue affecting the Controller Web UI login for some Controller on Cloud environments is resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding. Updated 05/19/2026 4:02 PM UTC Dear Customer, The issue affecting the Controller Web UI login for some Controller on Cloud environments has been identified, and the fix is currently being rolled out. Please expect the next update within the next 30 to 45 minutes. Thank you for your patience and understanding. Published 05/19/2026 12:46 PM UTC Dear Customer, We would like to inform you that some Controller on Cloud environments may currently experience issues with logging into the Controller Web UI. The Controller Client login remains unaffected and should continue to work as expected. Our teams are aware of the situation and are actively investigating the issue with high priority. We will provide the next update within the next 45 to 60 minutes. Thank you for your patience and understanding.

incident_updates.body: Updated 05/19/2026 5:43 PM UTC Dear Customer, The issue affecting the Controller Web UI login for some Controller on Cloud environments is resolved. recentIncidents.name: File Transfer Services & Drive Explorer Functionality Outage – EU Region
recentIncidents.body: Resolved 05/09/2026 1:36 AM UTC We have successfully re‑established the File Transfer service in our Frankfurt region. The service is now operational. Thank you for your continued patience as we complete the remaining recovery activities. Updated 05/08/2026 4:34 PM UTC Recovery efforts for the impacted services are ongoing. IBM teams are working around the clock, and we anticipate that restoration activities will continue over the next several days. Our immediate focus remains on restoring production environments. We are committed to keeping you informed with timely and transparent updates as progress is made. As more information becomes available, clients will receive environment-specific details directly through their existing support cases. Updated 05/08/2026 7:44 AM UTC NorthC continues to make progress toward restoring temporary power capabilities to initiate basic services at the Amsterdam data center. Site preparations are ongoing to support potential access, with IBM clearance still pending. There is no ETA for recovery at this point. Updated 05/08/2026 1:23 AM UTC The Amsterdam data center operator, NorthC, continues its assessment to determine the extent of damage, impact to the underlying power infrastructure, and associated repair timelines. The landlord's remediation and assessment activities are progressing as expected. Clearance for IBM to access the facility remains pending completion of NorthC's evaluation. Recovery is underway for services impacted. There is no ETA for recovery at this point Updated 05/07/2026 1:32 PM UTC Dear Customers, We would like to inform you that the File Transfer Service hosted in the Europe region is currently experiencing a service outage due to an ongoing incident at the Amsterdam data centre facility. This issue is impacting File Transfer Services and Drive Explorer functionality.Our teams are actively investigating the issue and working to restore services as quickly as possible while ensuring operational stability.We will continue to provide updates as more information becomes available. Thank you for your patience and understanding. Published 05/07/2026 12:15 PM UTC Dear Customers, We would like to inform you that the File Transfer Service hosted in the Europe region is currently experiencing a service outage due to an ongoing incident at the Amsterdam data centre facility. This issue is impacting File Transfer Services and Drive Explorer functionality. Our teams are actively investigating the issue and working to restore services as quickly as possible while ensuring operational stability. We will continue to provide updates as more information becomes available. Thank you for your patience and understanding.

recentIncidents.name: Amsterdam Outage Update
recentIncidents.body: Closed 05/16/2026 7:19 AM UTC Dear Customers, All Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud services impacted by the incident have now been fully restored, and the environment is operating normally. We continue to monitor and address any related issues that may arise. We appreciate your patience and understanding as our teams worked to safely recover services following the incident at the Amsterdam data centre. Throughout this event, our priority has been the safety of personnel and the restoration of services for our clients. If you continue to experience any issues, please update your existing support case or open a new case via the IBM Support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport This incident is now considered resolved, and no further updates will be posted to this Status Page. Updated 05/16/2026 7:16 AM UTC Dear Customers, All Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud services impacted by the incident have now been fully restored, and the environment is operating normally. We continue to monitor and address any related issues that may arise. We appreciate your patience and understanding as our teams worked to safely recover services following the incident at the Amsterdam data centre. Throughout this event, our priority has been the safety of personnel and the restoration of services for our clients. If you continue to experience any issues, please update your existing support case or open a new case via the IBM Support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport This incident is now considered resolved, and no further updates will be posted to this Status Page. Updated 05/15/2026 20:00 UTC We are making strong progress on Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud, with most client environments now restored. The remaining Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud environments that have not yet been restored, our engineering teams are continuing to work to restore these services. Moving forward, updates for clients awaiting restoration will be shared directly through open Support cases in the Support portal. Communications through this Status Page will no longer be used. If your environment has been restored and are experiencing any difficulties, please update your existing support case pertaining to the incident. If you do not currently have a case open, please open one via our support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport Updated 05/15/2026 16:00 UTC For Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud clients whose services have been restored, File Transfer capabilities are now available. The remaining Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud environments that have not yet been restored, our engineering teams are continuing to work to restore these services. A further status update will be provided at 20:00 UTC. If your environment has been restored and are experiencing any difficulties, please update your existing support case pertaining to the incident. If you do not currently have a case open, please open one via our support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport Updated 05/15/2026 12:57 UTC Service restoration is complete for most Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud environments, except for File Transfer functionality, which remains under recovery. For File Transfer and the remaining Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud environments that have not yet been restored, our engineering teams are continuing to work to restore these services. A further status update will be provided at 16:00 UTC. If your environment has been restored and are experiencing any difficulties, please update your existing support case pertaining to the incident. If you do not currently have a case open, please open one via our support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport Updated 05/15/2026 7:00 UTC Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud services have been restored for the majority of clients with exception of the File Transfer. File transfer and the remaining Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud clients are targeted to be operational by 12pm UTC, May 15. Clients will receive updates through their respective support cases. If your environment has been restored and are experiencing any difficulties, please update your existing support case pertaining to the incident. If you do not currently have a case open, please open one via our support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport Updated 05/15/2026 04:05 UTC Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud services have been restored for the majority of clients with exception of the File Transfer. File transfer and the remaining Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud clients are targeted to be operational by 12pm UTC, May 15. Clients will receive updates through their respective support cases. If your environment has been restored and are experiencing any difficulties, please update your existing support case pertaining to the incident. If you do not currently have a case open, please open one via our support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport Updated 05/14/2026 22:55 UTC IBM Cloud has successfully restored the required servers for Planning Analytics and Controller in the Amsterdam (AMS03) data center. We are now in the process of restoring services and will begin validation to ensure everything is operating as expected. We will keep clients informed of progress and next steps through their individual support cases as validation is completed. At this time, we expect client access to be fully restored by the end of the day on May 14th. Client environments that were previously restored in Frankfurt will continue to operate from there. Updated 05/14/2026 18:50 UTC IBM Cloud has successfully restored the required servers for Planning Analytics and Controller in the Amsterdam (AMS03) data center. We are now in the process of restoring services and will begin validation to ensure everything is operating as expected. We will keep clients informed of progress and next steps through their individual support cases as validation is completed. At this time, we expect client access to be fully restored by the end of the day on May 14th. Client environments that were previously restored in Frankfurt will continue to operate from there. Updated 05/14/2026 15:33 UTC IBM Cloud has successfully restored the required servers for Planning Analytics and Controller in the Amsterdam (AMS03) data center. We are now in the process of restoring services and will begin validation to ensure everything is operating as expected. We will keep clients informed of progress and next steps through their individual support cases as validation is completed. At this time, we expect client access to be fully restored by the end of the day on May 14th. Client environments that were previously restored in Frankfurt will continue to operate from there. Updated 05/14/2026 12:24 UTC IBM Cloud has successfully restored the required servers for Planning Analytics and Controller in the Amsterdam (AMS03) data center. We are now in the process of restoring services and will begin validation to ensure everything is operating as expected. We will keep clients informed of progress and next steps through their individual support cases as validation is completed. At this time, we expect client access to be fully restored by the end of the day on May 14th. Client environments that were previously restored in Frankfurt will continue to operate from there. Updated 05/14/2026 07:00 UTC Recovery activities for affected services continue to make strong progress as environments are being restored from Amsterdam to Frankfurt. IBM teams remain fully engaged and are working around the clock to accelerate recovery efforts. We currently expect restoration activities to continue over the next several days, during which additional clients will begin to see their services restored. We understand the critical nature of this incident and the impact it is having on your business operations. Teams across IBM remain fully mobilized globally and focused on restoring services as quickly and safely as possible. Additional technical and operational resources from across IBM have also been engaged to help accelerate recovery timelines. Planning Analytics Workspace (the multi-tenant component of Planning Analytics on Cloud) has now been fully restored. However, Planning Analytics services will not be fully operational until dedicated customer environments are provisioned and delivered. For clients utilizing both Controller and Planning Analytics, services will be restored together to ensure alignment and data consistency across environments. Individual support cases have been updated with estimated recovery timelines specific to each client environment and will continue to be updated regularly as additional information becomes available. For further details on IBM Cloud activities: https://cloud.ibm.com/status Updated 05/14/2026 03:00 UTC Recovery activities for affected services continue to make strong progress as environments are being restored from Amsterdam to Frankfurt. We encourage our clients to also monitor and subscribe to our status page for the service as this will be updated with current information as it becomes available. IBM teams are working around the clock, and we anticipate that restoration activities will continue over the next several days. Our immediate focus remains on restoring production environments. We are committed to keeping you informed with timely and transparent updates as progress is made. As more information becomes available, clients will receive environment-specific details directly through their existing support cases. Updated 05/08/2026 7:05 AM UTC NorthC continues to make progress toward restoring temporary power capabilities to initiate basic services at the Amsterdam data center. Site preparations are ongoing to support potential access, with IBM clearance still pending. There is no ETA for recovery at this point. Updated 05/08/2026 1:22 AM UTC The Amsterdam data center operator, NorthC, continues its assessment to determine the extent of damage, impact to the underlying power infrastructure, and associated repair timelines. The landlord's remediation and assessment activities are progressing as expected. Clearance for IBM to access the facility remains pending completion of NorthC's evaluation. Recovery is underway for services impacted. There is no ETA for recovery at this point Updated 05/07/2026 9:37 PM UTC IBM is aware of a fire at AMS03 data centre in Amsterdam which serves IBM. The facility has been evacuated, and there are no reported injuries. We are working closely with emergency services and assessing the impact on operations. Recovery is underway for services impacted. There is no ETA for recovery at this point. Another update will be provided in 4 hours. Updated 05/07/2026 5:01 PM UTC IBM is aware of a fire at AMS03 data centre in Amsterdam which serves IBM. The facility has been evacuated, and there are no reported injuries. We are working closely with emergency services and assessing the impact on operations. Recovery is underway for services impacted. There is no ETA for recovery at this point. Updated 05/07/2026 1:23 PM UTC Dear Customers, IBM is aware of a fire at a data centre in Amsterdam which serves IBM. The facility has been safely evacuated, and we can confirm that there are no reported injuries. We are working closely with emergency services and assessing the impact on operations. We will continue to share updates as more information becomes available. IBM Software SRE Planning Analytics on Cloud/Controller on Cloud Updated 05/07/2026 10:32 AM UTC Dear Customers, IBM is aware of a fire at a data centre in Amsterdam which serves IBM. The facility has been safely evacuated, and we can confirm that there are no reported injuries. We are working closely with emergency services and assessing the impact on operations. We will continue to share updates as more information becomes available. IBM Software SRE Planning Analytics on Cloud/Controller on Cloud Updated 05/07/2026 9:10 AM UTC Dear Customers, We would like to inform you that there is currently an incident being handled at the AMS03 data center. The relevant teams are actively investigating and taking necessary measures to ensure service continuity. We will continue to share updates as more information becomes available. Thank you for your understanding. Updated 05/07/2026 8:03 AM UTC Dear Customers, We would like to inform you that there is currently an incident being handled at the AMS03 data center. The relevant teams are actively investigating and taking necessary measures to ensure safety and service continuity. We will continue to share updates as more information becomes available. incident_updates.body: Closed 05/16/2026 7:19 AM UTC Dear Customers, All Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud services impacted by the incident have now been fully restored, and the environment is operating normally. We continue to monitor and address any related issues that may arise. We appreciate your patience and understanding as our teams worked to safely recover services following the incident at the Amsterdam data centre. Throughout this event, our priority has been the safety of personnel and the restoration of services for our clients. If you continue to experience any issues, please update your existing support case or open a new case via the IBM Support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport This incident is now considered resolved, and no further updates will be posted to this Status Page. Updated 05/16/2026 7:16 AM UTC Dear Customers, All Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud services impacted by the incident have now been fully restored, and the environment is operating normally. We continue to monitor and address any related issues that may arise. We appreciate your patience and understanding as our teams worked to safely recover services following the incident at the Amsterdam data centre. Throughout this event, our priority has been the safety of personnel and the restoration of services for our clients. If you continue to experience any issues, please update your existing support case or open a new case via the IBM Support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport This incident is now considered resolved, and no further updates will be posted to this Status Page. Updated 05/15/2026 20:00 UTC We are making strong progress on Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud, with most client environments now restored. The remaining Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud environments that have not yet been restored, our engineering teams are continuing to work to restore these services. Moving forward, updates for clients awaiting restoration will be shared directly through open Support cases in the Support portal. Communications through this Status Page will no longer be used. If your environment has been restored and are experiencing any difficulties, please update your existing support case pertaining to the incident. If you do not currently have a case open, please open one via our support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport Updated 05/15/2026 16:00 UTC For Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud clients whose services have been restored, File Transfer capabilities are now available. The remaining Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud environments that have not yet been restored, our engineering teams are continuing to work to restore these services. A further status update will be provided at 20:00 UTC. If your environment has been restored and are experiencing any difficulties, please update your existing support case pertaining to the incident. If you do not currently have a case open, please open one via our support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport Updated 05/15/2026 12:57 UTC Service restoration is complete for most Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud environments, except for File Transfer functionality, which remains under recovery. For File Transfer and the remaining Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud environments that have not yet been restored, our engineering teams are continuing to work to restore these services. A further status update will be provided at 16:00 UTC. If your environment has been restored and are experiencing any difficulties, please update your existing support case pertaining to the incident. If you do not currently have a case open, please open one via our support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport Updated 05/15/2026 7:00 UTC Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud services have been restored for the majority of clients with exception of the File Transfer. File transfer and the remaining Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud clients are targeted to be operational by 12pm UTC, May 15. Clients will receive updates through their respective support cases. If your environment has been restored and are experiencing any difficulties, please update your existing support case pertaining to the incident. If you do not currently have a case open, please open one via our support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport Updated 05/15/2026 04:05 UTC Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud services have been restored for the majority of clients with exception of the File Transfer. File transfer and the remaining Planning Analytics on Cloud and Controller on Cloud clients are targeted to be operational by 12pm UTC, May 15. Clients will receive updates through their respective support cases. If your environment has been restored and are experiencing any difficulties, please update your existing support case pertaining to the incident. If you do not currently have a case open, please open one via our support portal: https://www.ibm.com/mysupport Updated 05/14/2026 22:55 UTC IBM Cloud has successfully restored the required servers for Planning Analytics and Controller in the Amsterdam (AMS03) data center. We are now in the process of restoring services and will begin validation to ensure everything is operating as expected. We will keep clients informed of progress and next steps through their individual support cases as validation is completed. At this time, we expect client access to be fully restored by the end of the day on May 14th. Client environments that were previously restored in Frankfurt will continue to operate from there. Updated 05/14/2026 18:50 UTC IBM Cloud has successfully restored the required servers for Planning Analytics and Controller in the Amsterdam (AMS03) data center. We are now in the process of restoring services and will begin validation to ensure everything is operating as expected. We will keep clients informed of progress and next steps through their individual support cases as validation is completed. At this time, we expect client access to be fully restored by the end of the day on May 14th. Client environments that were previously restored in Frankfurt will continue to operate from there. We will keep clients informed of progress and next steps through their individual support cases as validation is completed. At this time, we expect client access to be fully restored by the end of the day on May 14th. Client environments that were previously restored in Frankfurt will continue to operate from there. Updated 05/14/2026 12:24 UTC IBM Cloud has successfully restored the required servers for Planning Analytics and Controller in the Amsterdam (AMS03) data center. We are now in the process of restoring services and will begin validation to ensure everything is operating as expected. We will keep clients informed of progress and next steps through their individual support cases as validation is completed. At this time, we expect client access to be fully restored by the end of the day on May 14th. Client environments that were previously restored in Frankfurt will continue to operate from there. Updated 05/14/2026 07:00 UTC Recovery activities for affected services continue to make strong progress as environments are being restored from Amsterdam to Frankfurt. IBM teams remain fully engaged and are working around the clock to accelerate recovery efforts. We currently expect restoration activities to continue over the next several days, during which additional clients will begin to see their services restored. We understand the critical nature of this incident and the impact it is having on your business operations. Teams across IBM remain fully mobilized globally and focused on restoring services as quickly and safely as possible. Additional technical and operational resources from across IBM have also been engaged to help accelerate recovery timelines. Planning Analytics Workspace (the multi-tenant component of Planning Analytics on Cloud) has now been fully restored. However, Planning Analytics services will not be fully operational until dedicated customer environments are provisioned and delivered. For clients utilizing both Controller and Planning Analytics, services will be restored together to ensure alignment and data consistency across environments. Individual support cases have been updated with estimated recovery timelines specific to each client environment and will continue to be updated regularly as additional information becomes available. For further details on IBM Cloud activities: https://cloud.ibm.com/status Updated 05/14/2026 03:00 UTC Recovery activities for affected services continue to make strong progress as environments are being restored from Amsterdam to Frankfurt.
